danger_signals.visual_form: Character transformation into Demonlord form; dark aura or supernatural visual effects on possessed fighters
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat; loss of human agency; irreversible evil state

danger_signals.visual_form: Gore animations during 'Death Combo' finishing moves; clothing/hair severing visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal threat; point of no return in combat; finality of defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Gladiatorial combat environment; single combatant facing successive opponents
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat; forced participation in lethal competition; no escape

safety_signals.visual_form: Shaman narrative framing; foretold destiny communicated through story text
safety_signals.meaning: Cosmic balance; prophesied champion exists; fate favors resistance to demon

safety_signals.visual_form: One-on-one duel structure; Demonlord accepts fair fight rather than assassinating children
safety_signals.meaning: Honor system in place; individual skill determines survival; exploitable weakness in enemy

safety_signals.visual_form: Spare/kill choice in Story Mode affecting endings; non-fatal paths available
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency over morality; redemption possible; alternative outcomes exist

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zarak as successor/usurper retains demonic aesthetic but distinct from Raith; progression shows consolidation of tyrannical power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament grounds controlled by Demonlord; arena structured to favor demon's supremacy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual weapon-based identities; diverse martial aesthetics reflecting various warrior cultures
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each of 7 characters has distinct visual design, armor, and weapon; no unified faction livery—suggest independent warriors rather than organized faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament participants; no permanent territorial or environmental control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: 'WeaponLord' title as mystical designation; shaman's blessing
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Protagonist character gains WeaponLord status through tournament victory; visual distinction through narrative rather than explicit costume change
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shaman's prediction as environmental/narrative marker of destiny; prophecy frames arena as battleground of fate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Established tyranny; 20 rulers already defeated and subjugated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demonlord refuses to kill child prophesied to oppose him—arrogance/honor code constrains action despite military advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power transition from Raith to Zarak; shift in demonic authority
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zarak holds tournament to identify and face the WeaponLord; testing of champions suggests uncertainty of demon's grip on power

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament arena as manifestation of prophecy's approach; fatalism in Demonlord's acceptance of duel
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zarak waits for champion rather than preemptively eliminating threats; cosmic forces override rational survival strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Victorious fighter's ending sequences vary based on moral choices (spare vs. kill opponents)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Branching narratives reflect world-state after prophecy's resolution—weaonlord becomes liberator or new tyrant based on player choices

environmental_storytelling.example: Shaman's foretelling ('When the night turns violent and the moon bleeds...') establishes atmospheric dread; moon imagery and temporal markers communicate inevitability

Demonlord Zarak's decision to hold public tournament rather than suppress dissent implies weakened control or deliberate confidence—visual hierarchy of arena (demon at top, challengers ascending) communicates political power structure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character sprite visual diversity as faction absence
environmental_storytelling.example: Seven fighters with distinct weapon aesthetics, armor styles, and body proportions (despite SNES sprite limitations) communicate independent mercenary status—no unified uniform/sigil suggests fragmented resistance rather than organized faction

environmental_storytelling.technique: Clothing/hair destruction as violence escalation signal
environmental_storytelling.example: Mid-combat severing of garments/hair (mechanic-visual fusion) communicates progression toward lethality; visual degradation of opponent matches mechanical approach to 'Death Combo' finality

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Refuses to kill prophesied child-killer despite lieutenants' counsel; accepts inevitable duel; arrogance masks anxiety about prophecy
Orchestrates public tournament; waits to face champion; behavior suggests both confidence and desire for legitimacy through fair combat rather than assassination

Participate in lethal tournament; willingly face demonic opponent; visual weapon expertise and varied martial styles suggest mercenary motivation or personal vendetta rather than coordinated resistance
Delivers prophecy without action; absence from combat arena communicates reliance on fate/player agency; mystical knowledge without direct intervention

notes: WeaponLord communicates political world-state primarily through narrative framing and tournament structure rather than detailed environmental visuals (limited by SNES sprite technology). The game's visual manifestation of danger relies heavily on gory fatality animations and demonic imagery rather than environmental hazards or faction-specific color palettes. The lack of unified faction aesthetics (except demonic entity) suggests a world of independent actors responding to prophecy-driven crisis rather than organized political conflict. Player moral choice in Story Mode (spare vs. kill defeated opponents) mechanically manifests different ending sequences, indicating world-state responds to player's ethical decisions. The prophecy mechanic serves as non-visual narrative world-state marker—all combat is framed as fulfillment of cosmic destiny, not strategic military campaign.
